perfect: I absolutely loved the Journal mechanic. It's incredible how the game solves the "what's the next step?" question so naturally.
The massive mystery and story created are very enjoyable.
The art design and its unique visual language perfectly complemented the game's melancholic yet intriguing atmosphere.
good: Traveling between planets with our spaceship and getting lost in that vast void was very enjoyable at first.
poor: I wish there were voice-overs in the dialogues; hearing that deep story from the characters' voices would have taken the atmosphere to a whole different level.
It took me some time to get used to the ship and character controls; they can feel clunky sometimes.
Having to walkthrough through similar explorations and paths repeatedly tried my patience after a certain point.
Although solving mysteries is a lot of fun, pure gameplay mechanics fall into repetition very easily and the "game" part falls behind the excitement of exploration.

mechanics.title: Journal
mechanics.text:
* A great system that completely eliminates the "what am I going to do now?" dilemma in the game.
* Following the question marks and solving that huge mystery piece by piece gave me an incredible sense of satisfaction.
* Even dying and starting everything from the beginning turned from being frustrating into a meaningful process thanks to this mechanic.

name: Outer Wilds

perfect: The art direction is simply breathtaking. The meticulous design of characters, environments, and equipment is so compelling that I often found myself just stopping to take it all in.
good: For the first time since Skyrim, I've truly felt that spark of joy while losing myself in an open-world RPG.
The game is brimming with a surprising amount of unique, handcrafted content.
poor: The main narrative feels somewhat lackluster and fails to leave a lasting impression.
Combat starts off with promise but quickly falters. Poor difficulty balancing turns enemies into tedious "bullet sponges" by the second chapter.
Frequent bugs, while rarely game-breaking, are persistent enough to occasionally sour the experience.
Side content begins to lose its charm and feels noticeably less polished starting from the second chapter.
The lack of an option to cancel active quests is a frustrating oversight.
Inventory management is cumbersome and frequently feels like a chore.
terrible: The AI is abysmal, consistently failing to create any sense of tactical or meaningful combat.
The game balance is a disaster. One moment you're an unstoppable force, the next you're being one-shotted. It's incredibly inconsistent across regions.
It took over seven grueling hours to find even remotely useful gear for my elemental mage build, and even now, the results feel underwhelming and unsatisfying.
name: Tainted Grail - The Fall of Avalon
hero: Game Atlas/Games/Tainted Grail - The Fall of Avalon/Sources/hero.jpg
thumbnail: Game Atlas/Games/Tainted Grail - The Fall of Avalon/Sources/thumbnail.jpg

perfect: I loved the art design. It felt like I was making pizza inside a hand-drawn fairy tale book.
good: The story progression and my interactions with the characters really hooked me.
The sound effects are incredibly satisfying.
The Garden mechanics were a great breathing point for me.
poor: The main gameplay loop started to bore me after a while. At some point, I kept playing just because I was curious about what would happen in the story.
The upgrades felt a bit insufficient to me. They generally just serve to automate the tasks I do and are not very creative.

terrible.text: I struggled a lot with User Experience (UX); it's full of choices that feel way behind today's standards and outdated.
children: For example, once I pick up the pizza cutter, there's no turning back; I have to finish that operation. Not being able to cancel even if I do something wrong stressed me out a lot.

terrible: Mobile game behaviors flow from every part of the PC version; in-game purchases (IAP) appearing at every corner ruined my enjoyment by giving a "free game" feeling.

I experienced a complete technical fiasco. Because of cloud save problems, the effort I put in for hours was deleted several times. The game becoming unplayable in this way was the last straw for me and I had to return it.

mechanics.title: Garden
mechanics.text:
* The garden mechanic brings a fresh breath to the game's routine pace.
* Adding the vegetables and fruits I grow myself to the pizzas is very enjoyable.

name: Good Pizza, Great Pizza
